text: Physics: Newtons second law F=ma relates force, mass, acceleration. Conservation of energy states energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed. Entropy always increases in isolated systems (second law of thermodynamics).

Computer Science: Big-O measures asymptotic growth rate. O(1)=constant lookup (hash table), O(log n)=binary search, O(n)=linear scan, O(n log n)=efficient sorting (merge/heap), O(n^2)=naive nested loops (bubble/insertion).

Mathematics: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus links differentiation and integration. If F(x)=integral from a to x of f(t)dt, then F'(x)=f(x). Integration undoes differentiation, enabling area computation via antiderivatives.

Biology: DNA replication is semi-conservative - each daughter gets one parental strand. Polymerase III adds dNTPs, primase makes RNA primers, ligase seals nicks. Leading strand is continuous, lagging uses Okazaki fragments (~200bp).

Chemistry: Le Chateliers principle - systems counteract disturbances. Pressure increase shifts to fewer gas moles. Catalyst speeds both directions equally. Temperature increase favors endothermic direction. pH = -log[H+].

Logic: Modus ponens - if P implies Q and P is true then Q is true. Modus tollens - if P implies Q and Q is false then P is false. Valid arguments preserve truth values. Sound arguments are valid with true premises.
